questions.text: Which word is a naming word (noun)?
questions.explanation: 'Garden' is a place, so it is a noun.

questions.text: Choose the noun: 'The dog barks loudly.'
questions.explanation: 'Dog' names an animal, so it is the noun.

questions.text: ____ is the name of a city.
questions.explanation: Mumbai is a city, so it is a noun.

questions.text: 'River' is a naming word.
questions.explanation: River names a thing in nature, so it is a noun. True.

questions.text: Which is an action word?
questions.explanation: 'Swim' is an action, so it is a verb.

questions.text: We ____ with our mouth.
questions.explanation: We eat with our mouth.

questions.text: The bird can ____.
questions.explanation: A bird can fly. 'Fly' is the action word.

questions.text: 'Sing' is an action word.
questions.explanation: Singing is an action, so 'sing' is a verb. True.

questions.text: Replace 'Riya' with a pronoun: 'Riya is happy.'
questions.explanation: Riya is a girl, so we use 'She'.

questions.text: Which pronoun is used for a book?
questions.explanation: A book is a thing, so we use 'It'.

questions.text: Ram and I are friends. ____ play together.
questions.explanation: For 'Ram and I' we use 'We'.

questions.text: We use 'He' for a boy.
questions.explanation: 'He' is used for a boy or man. True.

questions.text: Find the describing word: 'a tall tree'.
questions.explanation: 'Tall' describes the tree, so it is an adjective.

questions.text: Which word describes how something tastes?
questions.explanation: 'Sweet' is a describing word (adjective) for taste.

questions.text: The elephant is very ____.
questions.explanation: 'Big' describes the elephant, so it is an adjective.

questions.text: 'Beautiful' is a describing word.
questions.explanation: Beautiful describes a noun, so it is an adjective. True.

questions.text: Which sentence is about the present?
questions.explanation: 'I play now' is in the present tense.

questions.text: Yesterday I ____ to the park. (go)
questions.explanation: For the past (yesterday) we use 'went'.

questions.text: 'Tomorrow' tells us about the future.
questions.explanation: Tomorrow is future time. True.

questions.text: Opposite of 'fast' is ____.
questions.explanation: The opposite of fast is slow.

questions.text: Opposite of 'happy' is ____.
questions.explanation: The opposite of happy is sad.

questions.text: Opposite of 'old' is ____.
questions.explanation: The opposite of old is young/new.

questions.text: The opposite of 'full' is 'empty'.
questions.explanation: Full and empty are opposites. True.

questions.text: A group of cows is called a ____.
questions.explanation: A group of cows is called a herd.

questions.text: Which word rhymes with 'cat'?
questions.explanation: 'Hat' rhymes with 'cat'.

questions.text: A baby cat is called a ____.
questions.explanation: A baby cat is a kitten.

questions.text: 'Sun' and 'fun' rhyme.
questions.explanation: Sun and fun both end with 'un' sound — they rhyme. True.

questions.text: Choose the correct article: I saw ____ owl.
questions.explanation: Owl starts with a vowel sound, so we use 'an'.

questions.text: The cat is hiding ____ the bed (below it).
questions.explanation: Below the bed means 'under' the bed.

questions.text: The birds are sitting ____ the tree (on top).
questions.explanation: On top of the tree means 'on' the tree.

questions.text: Every sentence should start with a capital letter.
questions.explanation: Sentences begin with a capital letter. True.

questions.text: Which is a question?
questions.explanation: 'What is your name?' asks something, so it is a question.

questions.text: A question ends with a ____ mark.
questions.explanation: A question ends with a question mark (?).

questions.text: A 'doctor' is a person who treats sick people.
questions.explanation: A doctor treats sick people. True.
